question: Session 1 updates a row but has NOT committed. Session 2 tries to update the SAME row. What happens to session 2?
options: It overwrites the value right away
It waits (is blocked) until session 1 commits or rolls back
It reads the old value and proceeds

explanation.uz: 2-sessiya bloklanadi va kutadi: 1-sessiya qatorga exclusive (X) row lock qo'ygan. 1-sessiya COMMIT yoki ROLLBACK qilgach, 2-sessiya davom etadi.
explanation.en: Session 2 blocks and waits: session 1 holds an exclusive (X) row lock. Once session 1 commits or rolls back, session 2 proceeds.

question: What is 'blocking'?
options: Two sessions permanently stuck waiting on each other
One session waiting for a lock currently held by another session

explanation.uz: Blocking — bir sessiya boshqa sessiya ushlab turgan lockni kutishi. Bloklovchi sessiya COMMIT/ROLLBACK qilsa, kutuvchi davom etadi. (Deadlock esa o'zaro kutish.)
explanation.en: Blocking is one session waiting for a lock held by another. When the blocker commits/rolls back, the waiter proceeds. (A deadlock is mutual waiting.)

question: What is a DEADLOCK?
options: One session waiting for another to commit
Two or more sessions each holding a lock the other needs, so none can proceed

explanation.uz: Deadlock — ikki (yoki undan ko'p) sessiya bir-biri kutayotgan lockni ushlab turadi, natijada hech biri davom eta olmaydi (o'zaro bloklash).
explanation.en: A deadlock is two or more sessions each holding a lock the other is waiting for, so neither can proceed (mutual blocking).

question: How does Oracle handle a deadlock?
options: It automatically detects it and rolls back one statement (ORA-00060) to break the cycle

explanation.uz: Oracle deadlock'ni avtomatik aniqlaydi va tsiklni uzish uchun bitta sessiyaning statement'ini rollback qiladi (ORA-00060 xatosi). Tranzaksiya to'liq bekor bo'lmaydi — dastur qaror qabul qilishi kerak.
explanation.en: Oracle automatically detects the deadlock and rolls back one statement (ORA-00060) to break the cycle. The whole transaction isn't undone — the app must decide what to do.

question: When a session updates a row, what lock does it acquire on that row?
options: A lock on the whole tablespace

explanation.uz: DML qatorga exclusive (X) row lock oladi (va jadvalga shared TM lock). Shu sabab boshqa sessiya o'sha qatorni o'zgartira olmay kutadi, lekin boshqa qatorlar erkin.
explanation.en: DML takes an exclusive (X) row lock (plus a shared TM lock on the table). That's why another session can't modify that row until commit, while other rows stay free.

question: In Oracle, does a reader (SELECT) block a writer (UPDATE) on the same rows, or vice versa?
options: Yes, readers and writers block each other
No — readers don't block writers and writers don't block readers (multiversion read consistency)

explanation.uz: Oracle'ning asosiy tamoyili: o'quvchi yozuvchini, yozuvchi o'quvchini bloklamaydi. O'quvchi UNDO orqali oxirgi izchil versiyani ko'radi (multiversion read consistency).
explanation.en: A core Oracle principle: readers don't block writers and writers don't block readers. A reader sees the last consistent version via UNDO (multiversion read consistency).

question: What is the difference between a shared (S) lock and an exclusive (X) lock?
options: S allows others to read concurrently; X prevents others from modifying the resource until released

explanation.uz: Shared (S) lock bir vaqtda ko'p o'qishga ruxsat beradi. Exclusive (X) lock esa resursni faqat bitta sessiyaga beradi — boshqalar o'zgartira olmaydi (COMMIT/ROLLBACK gacha).
explanation.en: A shared (S) lock allows concurrent reads. An exclusive (X) lock grants the resource to one session — others can't modify it until it is released (commit/rollback).

question: What is a common application-design way to AVOID deadlocks?
options: Always access tables/rows in the same consistent order across all sessions

explanation.uz: Deadlock'ning oldini olishning oddiy yo'li — barcha sessiyalarda jadval/qatorlarga bir xil tartibda murojaat qilish. Shunda o'zaro kutish tsikli hosil bo'lmaydi.
explanation.en: A simple way to avoid deadlocks is to access tables/rows in the same consistent order in every session, so no waiting cycle can form.

question: What does SELECT ... FOR UPDATE do?
options: Nothing special — same as a normal SELECT
Locks the selected rows so other sessions can't update/delete them until you commit or roll back

explanation.uz: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE tanlangan qatorlarga lock qo'yadi — boshqa sessiyalar ularni COMMIT/ROLLBACK gacha o'zgartira/o'chira olmaydi. 'O'qib, keyin yangilash' stsenariylarida ishlatiladi.
explanation.en: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE locks the selected rows so other sessions can't update/delete them until you commit/rollback. Used for read-then-update scenarios.

question: Session 1 has an uncommitted UPDATE on a row. Session 2 runs SELECT on that same row. What does session 2 see?
options: The last committed version (via UNDO) — it does NOT wait

explanation.uz: 2-sessiya kutmaydi — u UNDO orqali oxirgi COMMIT qilingan versiyani ko'radi. Yozuvchi o'quvchini bloklamaydi.
explanation.en: Session 2 does not wait — it sees the last committed version via UNDO. A writer does not block a reader.
